text.title: Solution Assessment Framework
text.summary: Four assessment types, five-step evaluation process, assessment criteria categories, and triggers for each assessment horizon.

rule.statement: Select assessment type before gathering evidence; each type has a different question and artefact.
rule.rationale: Mixing assessment types produces evidence that answers the wrong question — testing deployment readiness with post-implementation metrics, or measuring business value during the design phase.

list.item: Design Assessment (during design): validate the proposed solution design against requirements.
Implementation Assessment (during build): verify correct implementation at sprint boundaries.
Deployment Assessment (at go-live): confirm readiness across QA, training, ops, support, security, compliance workstreams.
Post-Implementation Assessment (after deployment): measure actual value delivered against the original business case.

rule.statement: Run assessment as a five-step linear pipeline; do not skip or reorder steps.
rule.rationale: Step 3 (business value evaluation) depends on Step 1 criteria and Step 2 compliance data; skipping or reordering produces variance columns filled with storytelling rather than measurement.

list.item: Step 1 — Define criteria: business value, functional, performance, usability, compliance.
Step 2 — Assess against requirements: compare solution to each requirement, classify as met/partial/not met/deprecated.
Step 3 — Evaluate business value: measure delivered value against baseline, target, and variance.
Step 4 — Identify limitations: document gaps with impact and workaround columns.
Step 5 — Recommend action: accept / accept with conditions / require changes / reject.

rule.statement: Add "deprecated" as a first-class requirement status alongside met/partial/not met; do not force stale requirements into not-met.
rule.rationale: Forcing every legacy requirement written 9 months before go-live into met/not-met inflates the not-met count and wastes remediation tokens on requirements that were consciously retired.

list.item: Before go-live → Deployment readiness (gate: all workstreams ready/conditional/blocked with one evidence item each).
30 days post go-live → Initial post-implementation assessment.
90 days post go-live → Full post-implementation assessment.

rule.statement: Route the assessment through someone whose compensation does not depend on the verdict; do not let the project sponsor's team self-assess.
rule.rationale: BABOK Solution Evaluation explicitly requires independence; assessments conducted by teams whose bonuses depend on the outcome systematically overstate value realization.

text.reference: Full assessment report template with requirements compliance, business value, and recommendations.
Post-go-live review covering business outcomes, adoption metrics, and lessons learned.
